# kubernetes.recipes — Complete Recipe Index

> 1373 Kubernetes recipes and guides

## AI & GPU (233 recipes)

- [Agentic AI and Multi-Agent Systems](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/agentic-ai-multiagent-kubernetes/): Deploy autonomous AI agents and multi-agent orchestration on Kubernetes. LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, tool-calling agents, agent-to-agent communication.
- [Volcano Job minAvailable Gang Scheduling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-batch-processing-volcano/): Configure Volcano job minAvailable for gang scheduling on Kubernetes. Batch AI training, fair-share queues, job plugins, and GPU preemption guide.
- [AI Content Watermarking on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-content-watermarking-kubernetes/): Deploy AI-generated content watermarking on Kubernetes. Invisible watermarks, SynthID integration, detection APIs, image and text watermarking pipelines.
- [AI Infrastructure Cost Optimization](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-infrastructure-cost-optimization-kubernetes/): Optimize AI infrastructure costs on Kubernetes. GPU sharing, spot instances, inference batching, model quantization, token economics.
- [AI-Native Development Platforms on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-native-development-kubernetes/): Build AI-native development platforms on Kubernetes. AI coding agents, automated testing, Copilot infrastructure, dev containers, and AI-driven CI/CD pipelines.
- [AI Resource Allocation Optimization](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-resource-allocation-optimization-kubernetes/): Optimize GPU and memory allocation for AI workloads on Kubernetes. Right-size GPU requests, bin-packing strategies, gang scheduling.
- [AI Supercomputing on Kubernetes GPU Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-supercomputing-kubernetes-gpu-clusters/): Build AI supercomputing platforms on Kubernetes. Multi-node GPU training, NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, InfiniBand RDMA, NCCL tuning, Blackwell clusters.
- [AIPerf Benchmark LLMs on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-benchmark-llm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA AIPerf to benchmark LLM inference performance on Kubernetes. Measure TTFT, ITL, throughput with real-time dashboard and GPU telemetry.
- [AIPerf Concurrency Sweep on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-concurrency-sweep-kubernetes/): Run AIPerf concurrency sweeps on Kubernetes to find optimal LLM serving capacity. Automate 1-128 concurrent user benchmarks with batch Jobs.
- [AIPerf Goodput and SLO Benchmarks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-goodput-slo-benchmark/): Measure LLM goodput with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Define SLOs for TTFT and ITL, calculate effective throughput, and benchmark with timeslice analysis.
- [AIPerf LLM Benchmarking on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-llm-benchmarking-kubernetes/): Benchmark generative AI inference on Kubernetes with NVIDIA AIPerf. Measure TTFT, ITL, throughput, and latency across vLLM, NIM.
- [AIPerf Multi-Model Benchmark on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-multi-model-benchmark/): Compare multiple LLM models and backends with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Benchmark vLLM vs TGI vs Triton with automated multi-run confidence intervals.
- [AIPerf Trace Replay Benchmarks on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-trace-replay-kubernetes/): Replay production traffic traces with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Use moon_cake format, ShareGPT datasets, and fixed schedules for realistic LLM benchmarks.
- [AIPerf Offline vLLM Benchmarking](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-vllm-benchmark-offline/): Benchmark vLLM inference with AIPerf in air-gapped Kubernetes clusters. Use dummy tokenizers, offline mode, custom endpoints.
- [Autonomous Industrial Systems on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/autonomous-industrial-systems-kubernetes/): Orchestrate autonomous factories and logistics with Kubernetes. Digital twins, robot fleet coordination, industrial IoT pipelines, predictive maintenance.
- [CNCF AI Projects Landscape Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/cnai-cncf-ai-projects-landscape/): Navigate the CNCF AI project landscape for Kubernetes. Kubeflow, KServe, KAITO, Volcano, and emerging projects for training, serving, scheduling.
- [Compare NCCL Intra-Node vs Inter-Node Perfo...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/compare-nccl-intra-inter-node/): Build a repeatable comparison between local and cross-node NCCL throughput to validate GPU cluster interconnect scaling and identify bottlenecks early.
- [Continuous Batching LLM Inference K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/continuous-batching-llm-kubernetes/): Configure continuous batching for LLM inference on Kubernetes. vLLM and TRT-LLM batch scheduling, max-num-seqs tuning, and throughput optimization.
- [Copy NVIDIA NIM Images to Internal Quay Reg...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/copy-nim-image-internal-quay-registry/): Pull NIM container images from nvcr.io and push to an internal Quay registry. Covers authentication, tagging, air-gapped workflows, and curl token issues.
- [CUDA Version Compatibility K8s Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/cuda-compatibility-kubernetes-guide/): Match CUDA versions with GPU drivers and container images on Kubernetes. Forward compatibility, driver requirements, and container toolkit matrix.
- [Deep Learning with Large Datasets on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deep-learning-large-dataset-kubernetes/): Optimize deep learning training with large datasets on Kubernetes. Covers data loading, caching strategies, parallel prefetch, and storage architecture
- [DeepSpeed ZeRO Training Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deepspeed-kubernetes-distributed/): Deploy DeepSpeed ZeRO-1/2/3 for large model training on Kubernetes. Multi-node config, NCCL tuning, memory optimization, and 70B+ model training.
- [Dell PowerEdge XE7740 GPU Node Setup](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/dell-poweredge-xe7740-gpu-setup/): Configure Dell PowerEdge XE7740 GPU nodes with H200 GPUs for OpenShift and Kubernetes including BIOS, power, cooling, and network setup.
- [Deploy Fish Audio TTS on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-fish-audio-tts-kubernetes/): Deploy Fish Audio S2-Pro 5B text-to-speech model on Kubernetes for high-quality voice synthesis with multi-speaker support and streaming audio.
- [Deploy GLM-5 754B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-glm5-754b-kubernetes/): Deploy Zhipu AI GLM-5 754B model on Kubernetes with vLLM. One of the largest open-weight models with multi-node tensor parallelism across 8+ GPUs.
- [Deploy Granite 4.0 Speech on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-granite-speech-kubernetes/): Deploy IBM Granite 4.0 1B Speech model on Kubernetes for automatic speech recognition. Lightweight 2B model runs on CPU or small GPU for STT workloads.
- [Deploy Kimi K2.5 1.1T MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-kimi-k25-kubernetes/): Deploy Moonshot AI Kimi-K2.5 1.1T MoE multimodal model on Kubernetes. The largest open MoE model with 2.69M downloads for frontier AI tasks.
- [Deploy Llama 2 70B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-llama2-70b-kubernetes/): Deploy Meta Llama 2 70B on Kubernetes with multi-GPU tensor parallelism, vLLM serving, and production-ready health checks and resource limits.
- [Deploy Llama 3.1 8B Instruct on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-llama31-8b-kubernetes/): Deploy Meta Llama 3.1 8B Instruct on Kubernetes with vLLM. Production-ready single-GPU deployment with 128K context, tool calling, and autoscaling.
- [Deploy LTX Video Generation on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-ltx-video-kubernetes/): Deploy Lightricks LTX-2.3 image-to-video model on Kubernetes for AI video generation with batch processing and S3 output storage.
- [Deploy MiniMax M2.5 229B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-minimax-m25-kubernetes/): Deploy MiniMax M2.5 229B model on Kubernetes with vLLM. High-performance LLM with 485K downloads, optimized for multi-turn conversation and long context.
- [Deploy Mistral 7B with NVIDIA NIM](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-mistral-nvidia-nim/): Step-by-step guide to deploy Mistral-7B using NVIDIA NIM with TensorRT-LLM backend on Kubernetes for optimized GPU inference.
- [Deploy Mistral 7B with vLLM on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-mistral-vllm-kubernetes/): Step-by-step guide to deploy Mistral-7B-v0.1 using vLLM as an OpenAI-compatible inference server on Kubernetes with GPU fractioning.
- [Deploy Multinode NIM Models on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-multinode-nim-models-kubernetes/): Run large language models across multiple GPU nodes with NVIDIA NIM. Tensor parallelism, NCCL, InfiniBand, and Kubernetes Job orchestration.
- [Deploy NVIDIA Nemotron 120B MoE on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-nemotron-120b-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B MoE model on Kubernetes. 120B total parameters with 12B active for enterprise-grade inference.
- [Deploy Microsoft Phi-4 on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-phi4-kubernetes/): Deploy Microsoft Phi-4 small language model on Kubernetes with vLLM. Efficient 14B model with GPT-4 level reasoning on a single GPU.
- [Deploy Phi-4 Reasoning Vision on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-phi4-reasoning-vision-kubernetes/): Deploy Microsoft Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B on Kubernetes for multimodal chain-of-thought reasoning with visual understanding on a single GPU.
- [Deploy Qwen3 235B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-235b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3-235B-A22B mixture-of-experts model on Kubernetes. Only 22B parameters active per token for efficient 235B-class inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3 Coder 80B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-coder-kubernetes/): Deploy Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B on Kubernetes for code generation, review, and refactoring. Production-ready AI coding assistant with multi-GPU serving.
- [Deploy Qwen3 TTS on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-tts-kubernetes/): Deploy Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice on Kubernetes for text-to-speech with custom voice cloning. 1.13M downloads, lightweight single-GPU deployment.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 35B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-35b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-35B-A3B mixture-of-experts multimodal model on Kubernetes. 35B total parameters with only 3B active for ultra-efficient inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 397B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-397b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-397B-A17B MoE multimodal model on Kubernetes. 397B total parameters with only 17B active per token for frontier VLM inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 9B Multimodal on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-9b-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-9B vision-language model on Kubernetes with vLLM. Process images and text with a single GPU deployment.
- [RetinaNet Object Detection on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-retinanet-kubernetes/): Deploy RetinaNet object detection model on Kubernetes with Triton Inference Server, TensorRT optimization, and batch processing pipelines.
- [Deploy Sarvam 105B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-sarvam-105b-kubernetes/): Deploy Sarvam 105B multilingual LLM on Kubernetes with vLLM. India's largest open language model with native support for 10+ Indic languages.
- [Stable Diffusion XL on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-stable-diffusion-xl-kubernetes/): Deploy Stable Diffusion XL for image generation on Kubernetes with TensorRT acceleration, queued batch processing, and S3 output storage.
- [Deploy Whisper Speech-to-Text on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-whisper-kubernetes/): Deploy OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text on Kubernetes with faster-whisper, batch transcription Jobs, and real-time streaming endpoints.
- [DGX H100 GPU Topology nvidia-smi](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/dgx-h100-gpu-topology-nvidia-smi/): Inspect DGX H100 GPU topology with nvidia-smi topo -m. NVSwitch NV18 links, cross-socket detection, PCIe hierarchy, and NCCL performance validation.
- [DGX H100 nvidia-smi topo -m Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/dgx-h100-nvidia-smi-topo-kubernetes/): Read nvidia-smi topo -m output on DGX H100 systems. Understand NVLink, NVSwitch, PCIe topology, GPU-to-GPU bandwidth, and NUMA affinity for Kubernetes.
- [Disable PCIe ACS for GPU-Direct P2P](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/disable-acs-pcie-gpu-direct-p2p/): Disable PCIe Access Control Services (ACS) to enable GPU-Direct peer-to-peer DMA between GPUs and RDMA NICs. Covers BIOS disable, kernel override, and when
- [Disable GDS and Enable IOMMU Passthrough on K8s GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/disable-gds-enable-iommu-passthrough-kubernetes/): Disable GPUDirect Storage (GDS) when not needed and configure IOMMU passthrough mode for GPU and NIC device assignment. Kernel parameters, BIOS settings, VFIO
- [DisaggregatedSet for Multi-Role LLM Inference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/disaggregatedset-leaderworkerset-llm-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy disaggregated LLM inference on Kubernetes with DisaggregatedSet and LeaderWorkerSet. Separate prefill and decode phases across GPU pools
- [Distributed Inference Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy distributed LLM inference with tensor parallelism across multiple GPUs and pipeline parallelism across nodes on Kubernetes.
- [Distributed Inference with Run:ai](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-inference-runai-kubernetes/): Deploy distributed AI inference with NVIDIA Run:ai on Kubernetes. Single-node Knative, multinode LeaderWorkerSet, NIM, autoscaling, and observability.
- [Distributed Multi-GPU Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-multi-gpu-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy distributed inference across multiple GPUs and nodes on Kubernetes. Covers tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism, vLLM, and NIM multi-GPU serving.
- [Distributed Training TensorFlow PyTorch](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-training-tensorflow-pytorch-kubernetes/): Run distributed training jobs on Kubernetes with TensorFlow and PyTorch. Training Operator, multi-worker strategies, NCCL configuration.
- [DOCA Perftest RDMA Benchmarking](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/doca-perftest-rdma-benchmark-kubernetes/): Run NVIDIA DOCA perftest on Kubernetes to benchmark RDMA bandwidth and latency between GPU nodes. Traffic patterns, GPUDirect memory modes.
- [Domain-Specific Language Models on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/domain-specific-language-models-kubernetes/): Deploy and fine-tune domain-specific LLMs on Kubernetes. Legal, healthcare, finance, and code models with LoRA fine-tuning, NIM serving, and RAG pipelines.
- [Feast Feature Store Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/feast-feature-store-kubernetes/): Deploy Feast feature store on Kubernetes for ML feature management. Offline and online stores, feature serving, point-in-time joins.
- [FSDP LoRA Fine-Tuning LLMs on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/fsdp-lora-finetuning-kubernetes/): Fine-tune large language models with FSDP and LoRA on Kubernetes. Covers memory-efficient loading, checkpoint strategies, and multi-node H200 training.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmark LLM Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-benchmark-kubernetes/): Benchmark LLM inference with GenAI-Perf on Kubernetes. Use --service-kind openai for vLLM, NIM, and TGI. Measure TTFT, ITL, and throughput.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmark LLM Serving](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-benchmark-llm/): Benchmark LLM inference endpoints with NVIDIA GenAI-Perf for throughput, latency percentiles, time-to-first-token, and ITL metrics.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmarking LLM Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-benchmarking-llm-inference-kubernetes/): Benchmark LLM inference performance with NVIDIA GenAI-Perf on Kubernetes. Profile vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, and Triton endpoints with concurrency sweeps, token
- [NVIDIA GenAI-Perf Inference Benchmarking](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-nvidia-inference-benchmarking/): Benchmark LLM inference throughput and latency on Kubernetes using NVIDIA GenAI-Perf. Covers vLLM, Run:ai, concurrency testing, and multi-location client runs.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmark Triton on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-triton-benchmark/): Benchmark NVIDIA Triton Inference Server performance on Kubernetes using GenAI-Perf. Measure TTFT, inter-token latency, throughput, and GPU telemetry.
- [GitOps for AI Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gitops-ai-workloads-kubernetes/): Deploy AI models with GitOps on Kubernetes. Version ML models in Git, ArgoCD for model rollouts, Flux for GPU cluster sync.
- [Enable GPUDirect Storage in ClusterPolicy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-clusterpolicy-gds-enable/): Enable NVIDIA GPUDirect Storage (GDS) in the GPU Operator ClusterPolicy for direct GPU-to-NVMe data paths. Driver module configuration and verification.
- [GPU Feature Discovery Node Labels](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-feature-discovery-kubernetes/): Configure NVIDIA GPU Feature Discovery for automatic node labeling on Kubernetes. GPU model, driver version, CUDA, and MIG labels for scheduling.
- [GPU Node Affinity Scheduling K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-node-affinity-scheduling/): Schedule GPU workloads with node affinity and topology on Kubernetes. GPU type selection, multi-GPU locality, and NUMA-aware pod placement.
- [GPU Operator ClusterPolicy RDMA and GDS Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-operator-clusterpolicy-rdma-gds-iommu/): Configure NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy to disable RDMA and enable GPUDirect Storage (GDS). Control nvidia-peermem, nvidia-fs modules, driver
- [GPU Sharing MIG and Time-Slicing Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-sharing-mig-timeslicing-kubernetes/): Share GPUs across multiple pods with NVIDIA MIG and time-slicing on Kubernetes. MIG profiles for A100/H100, time-slicing configuration.
- [GPU Time-Slicing on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-time-slicing-kubernetes/): Share GPUs across multiple workloads using NVIDIA time-slicing on Kubernetes. Configure the device plugin, set replica counts, and manage fairness.
- [GPUDirect RDMA Setup and Verification on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpudirect-rdma-setup-verification-kubernetes/): Enable and verify GPUDirect RDMA for GPU-to-NIC direct data transfer on Kubernetes. Install nvidia-peermem, configure DMA-BUF, verify RDMA paths, troubleshoot
- [GPUDirect Storage on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpudirect-storage-gds-kubernetes/): Configure NVIDIA GPUDirect Storage (GDS) for direct data path between NVMe/NFS storage and GPU memory bypassing CPU. Covers Magnum IO, cuFile API, GDS driver
- [Hermes Agent Self-Hosted AI on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/hermes-agent-self-hosted-kubernetes/): Deploy Hermes Agent (Nous Research) on Kubernetes as a persistent self-hosted AI agent with memory, automated skill creation, multi-platform
- [Integrate DisaggregatedSet with llm-d on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/integrate-disaggregatedset-llmd-kubernetes/): Deploy disaggregated LLM inference using DisaggregatedSet and llm-d on Kubernetes. Install LWS then DS controller, model prefill/decode roles, wire llm-d
- [Inter-Node Tensor Parallelism on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/inter-node-tensor-parallelism-kubernetes/): Split a single LLM across multiple physical servers using tensor parallelism. Configure vLLM, NIM, and Ray for inter-node TP with NCCL over RDMA or TCP.
- [IOMMU BIOS and Kernel Config for NCCL GPU-Direct](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/iommu-bios-kernel-nccl-gpu-direct/): Configure IOMMU at BIOS and kernel level to enable NCCL GPU-Direct RDMA on Kubernetes. Covers Intel VT-d, AMD-Vi, kernel parameters, passthrough
- [IOMMU Kernel Parameters for Kubernetes GPU Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/iommu-kernel-parameters-gpu-nodes-kubernetes/): Configure IOMMU kernel parameters for optimal GPU and RDMA performance on Kubernetes. Compare intel_iommu, amd_iommu, and iommu settings, passthrough vs off vs
- [GPU Sharing and Bin Packing with KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-gpu-sharing/): Maximize GPU utilization with KAI Scheduler GPU sharing, fractional GPUs, and bin packing strategies for Kubernetes AI workloads.
- [Installing NVIDIA KAI Scheduler AI Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-installation/): Deploy KAI Scheduler for optimized GPU resource allocation in Kubernetes AI/ML clusters with hierarchical queues and batch scheduling
- [Batch Scheduling PodGroups in KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-podgroups/): Implement gang scheduling for distributed training jobs using KAI Scheduler PodGroups to ensure all-or-nothing pod scheduling
- [Hierarchical Queues & Resource Fairness KAI...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-queues/): Configure hierarchical queues in KAI Scheduler for multi-tenant GPU clusters with quotas, limits, and Dominant Resource Fairness (DRF)
- [Topology-Aware Scheduling with KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-topology-aware/): Optimize GPU workload placement using KAI Scheduler's Topology-Aware Scheduling (TAS) for NVLink, NVSwitch, and disaggregated serving architectures
- [KAITO AI Model Inference Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kaito-ai-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy AI models with KAITO (Kubernetes AI Toolchain Operator) for automated GPU provisioning, model serving, and inference workload management.
- [Katib Hyperparameter Tuning Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/katib-hyperparameter-tuning-kubernetes/): Automate hyperparameter tuning with Katib on Kubernetes. Bayesian optimization, random search, grid search, early stopping.
- [KnativeServing for AI Inference OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/knative-serving-nim-inference-openshift/): Configure KnativeServing with scale-to-zero, GPU scheduling features, Kourier ingress, and custom domain templates for AI inference workloads on OpenShift.
- [KServe Model Serving Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kserve-model-serving-kubernetes/): Deploy ML models with KServe for serverless inference on Kubernetes. InferenceService, scale-to-zero, canary rollouts, model transformers.
- [Distrib. Training Kubeflow Training Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-distributed-training/): Run multi-node distributed PyTorch and TensorFlow training jobs using Kubeflow Training Operator with NCCL, RDMA, and shared storage.
- [Kubeflow ML Platform Setup Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-ml-platform-kubernetes/): Deploy Kubeflow as a production-ready ML platform on Kubernetes. Notebooks, pipelines, training operators, and model serving with KServe for end-to-end MLO.
- [Kubeflow MPIJob Worker SSH Setup for GPU Training](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-mpijob-worker-ssh-gpu-training/): Configure SSH daemon in Kubeflow MPIJob worker pods for multi-node GPU training. Covers SSHD setup in containers, host key generation, authorized keys from MPI
- [Kubeflow Operator: Full ML Platform](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-operator-platform/): Deploy the complete Kubeflow platform on Kubernetes with the Kubeflow Operator. Covers Pipelines, Notebooks, KServe, Katib, and multi-tenant ML workflows.
- [Kubeflow Training Operator on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-training-operator/): Install Kubeflow Training Operator for distributed ML training with PyTorchJob, TFJob, and MPIJob on GPU-enabled Kubernetes clusters.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 DRA for GPU and TPU Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-1-36-dra-gpu-management/): Use Dynamic Resource Allocation in Kubernetes 1.36 for advanced GPU/TPU management with partitionable devices, device taints, and tolerations.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 Gang Scheduling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-1-36-gang-scheduling/): Use gang scheduling in Kubernetes 1.36 to schedule Pod groups atomically. Essential for distributed ML training, MPI jobs, and Spark workloads.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 RestartAllContainers for ML](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-1-36-restart-all-containers/): Use the RestartAllContainers policy in Kubernetes 1.36 to restart all Pod containers in-place when a worker fails, avoiding costly ML training rescheduling.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 Topology-Aware Scheduling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-1-36-topology-aware-scheduling/): Use topology-aware workload scheduling in Kubernetes 1.36 to place Pods on nodes with optimal GPU, NUMA, and network topology for ML training.
- [AI Cost Management on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-cost-management/): Control AI infrastructure costs on Kubernetes with GPU utilization tracking, chargeback per team, spot instance strategies, right-sizing recommendations.
- [K8s AI Gateway: Inference Extension Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-gateway-inference-extension/): Use the Kubernetes AI Gateway and Inference Extension to route LLM traffic. Model-aware routing, load balancing across inference backends.
- [AI Inference Optimization Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-inference-optimization/): Optimize AI inference performance on Kubernetes. Request batching, KV cache tuning, speculative decoding, continuous batching.
- [Kubernetes AI Infrastructure Scaling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-infrastructure-scaling/): Scale AI inference infrastructure on Kubernetes from 10K to 100K requests per second. Covers latency optimization, horizontal scaling, caching
- [Kubernetes for AI Search and Discoverability](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-search-discoverability/): Deploy AI-searchable services on Kubernetes: llms.txt implementation, RAG-optimized APIs, structured data for AI chatbots, and infrastructure patterns
- [Dynamic Resource Allocation for GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-dynamic-resource-allocation-gpu/): Use Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation to schedule GPUs. DRA ResourceClaims, partitionable devices, GPU sharing, and structured parameters for accelerators.
- [K8s GPU Limits Requests Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-limits-requests/): Configure GPU resource limits and requests in Kubernetes pod specs. nvidia.com/gpu resource, fractional GPUs, MIG slices, and multi-GPU allocation.
- [GPU Node Provisioning Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-node-provisioning/): Automate GPU node provisioning for Kubernetes with Karpenter, Cluster Autoscaler, and cloud-specific node pools for AI and ML workloads.
- [GPU Operator Advanced Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-operator-advanced-config/): Advanced NVIDIA GPU Operator configuration on Kubernetes. Driver containers, CUDA toolkit, GDS, GPUDirect RDMA, MIG manager, DCGM Exporter.
- [GPU Sharing with MPS and MIG on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-sharing-mps-mig/): Share NVIDIA GPUs across multiple pods using MPS time-slicing and MIG hardware partitioning. Maximize GPU utilization for inference workloads.
- [Kubeflow PyTorchJob Training K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-kubeflow-pytorchjob/): Run distributed PyTorch training on Kubernetes with Kubeflow PyTorchJob. ElasticPolicy, nproc_per_node, RDMA configuration, and multi-GPU scaling.
- [Kubernetes Topology Manager for GPU and NUMA Alignment](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-topology-manager-gpu-numa/): Configure Kubernetes Topology Manager to align CPU, GPU, and NIC allocations on the same NUMA node. Covers policies, kubelet config, and GPU performance tuning.
- [Kueue for Batch Jobs and GPU Queues](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kueue-batch-gpu-jobs-kubernetes/): Use Kueue to manage batch job queues on Kubernetes. GPU quota, fair sharing, priority queues, ML training workloads, and multi-tenant cluster scheduling.
- [Kueue Job Queuing Fair Sharing Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kueue-job-queuing-kubernetes/): Implement fair-share GPU job queuing with Kueue on Kubernetes. ClusterQueues, LocalQueues, ResourceFlavors, and cohort-based borrowing for multi-team AI cl.
- [LeaderWorkerSet Operator for AI Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/leaderworkerset-operator/): Deploy distributed AI training with LeaderWorkerSet Operator on Kubernetes and OpenShift for leader-worker topology with gang scheduling.
- [Llama 2 70B FP16 Model Size 140GB Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llama-70b-model-size-gpu-requirements/): Llama 2 70B FP16 model size is 140GB. Complete GPU memory requirements for FP16, FP8, INT4 quantization, and multi-GPU tensor parallelism on Kubernetes.

- [Test LLM Inference Endpoints with curl](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/test-llm-inference-endpoints/): Validate Kubernetes-hosted LLM inference services using curl against OpenAI-compatible /v1/models, /v1/completions, and /v1/chat/completions endpoints.
- [Time-Slicing vs MIG vs Full GPU Allocation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/timeslicing-mig-full-gpu/): Compare GPU sharing strategies: time-slicing for notebooks, MIG for isolated inference, and full GPU for training workloads.
- [Triton Autoscaling with GPU Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-autoscaling-gpu-metrics/): Autoscale Triton Inference Server on Kubernetes using GPU utilization, request queue depth, and inference latency metrics with KEDA and HPA.
- [Triton Inference Server vs vLLM: Which to C...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-inference-server-vs-vllm-comparison/): Compare NVIDIA Triton Inference Server vs vLLM for LLM serving on Kubernetes. Performance, multi-model support, batching, GPU utilization.
- [Triton Multi-Model Serving on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-multi-model-serving/): Serve multiple LLMs simultaneously on Triton Inference Server using TensorRT-LLM and vLLM backends with model routing and GPU scheduling.
- [Triton TensorRT-LLM on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-tensorrt-llm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Triton Inference Server with TensorRT-LLM backend on Kubernetes for optimized large language model serving with GPU acceleration.
- [TensorRT-LLM vs vLLM on Triton](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-tensorrt-vllm-comparison/): Compare TensorRT-LLM and vLLM backends on Triton Inference Server. When to use each, performance benchmarks, and migration strategies.
- [Triton with vLLM Backend on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-vllm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Triton Inference Server with vLLM backend on Kubernetes for flexible LLM serving with PagedAttention and continuous batching.
- [Deploying Vector Databases on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vector-database-kubernetes/): Deploy and operate vector databases (Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant) on Kubernetes for RAG pipelines, semantic search, and AI applications with persistent.
- [Verify NCCL RDMA Traffic with Debug Logging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/verify-nccl-rdma-traffic-debug/): Prove NCCL uses RDMA for GPU communication on Kubernetes. Use NCCL_DEBUG and NCCL_DEBUG_SUBSYS=ALL to verify InfiniBand, RoCE.
- [vLLM Alternatives LLM Inference K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-alternatives-llm-inference-k8s/): Compare vLLM alternatives for LLM inference on Kubernetes. TensorRT-LLM, SGLang, NVIDIA NIM, Ollama, and text-generation-inference feature comparison.
- [vLLM on Huawei Ascend NPU: K8s Deployment](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-ascend-npu-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy vLLM inference on Huawei Ascend NPUs in Kubernetes. Atlas 300I/910B device plugin, vllm-ascend container image, tensor parallelism, and model serving.
- [Debug Distributed vLLM Inference with NCCL Verbose Logging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-distributed-inference-debugging-nccl/): Debug distributed vLLM inference using NCCL_DEBUG=INFO and NCCL_DEBUG_SUBSYS=ALL. Covers air-gapped deployment with TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE, interpreting NCCL
- [vLLM Deployment Kubernetes Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-kubernetes-deployment-guide/): Deploy vLLM inference engine on K8s. Model loading, tensor parallelism, continuous batching, and OpenAI-compatible API setup.
- [Deploy vLLM OpenAI Container on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-openai-container-kubernetes/): Deploy the vLLM OpenAI-compatible server container on Kubernetes. Pull ghcr.io/vllm-project/vllm-openai, configure GPU resources, model loading.
- [Kubernetes Volcano Batch Scheduler Gang Scheduling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/volcano-batch-scheduler-gang-scheduling-kubernetes/): Deploy Volcano batch scheduler for gang scheduling on Kubernetes. - [Volcano Job minAvailable Gang Schedule](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/volcano-batch-scheduling-kubernetes/): Volcano batch scheduling with minAvailable gang scheduling on Kubernetes. Job configuration, queue policies, and AI training workload scheduling.
- [Weights and Biases Experiment Tracking on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/weights-and-biases-experiment-tracking-kubernetes/): Deploy Weights & Biases (W&B) on Kubernetes for ML experiment tracking, model registry, and hyperparameter sweeps. Self-hosted W&B Server, agent-based

## Autoscaling (46 recipes)

- [HPA Container Resource Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-container-resource-metrics/): Configure HPA to scale based on individual container metrics instead of pod-level averages. Target specific containers in multi-container pods.

## Configuration (216 recipes)

- [Migrate from gitRepo Volume in Kubernetes 1.36](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-1-36-gitrepo-removal/): The gitRepo volume plugin is permanently removed in Kubernetes 1.36. Migrate to init containers or OCI volumes to avoid broken deployments.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 Graceful Leader Transition](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-1-36-graceful-leader-transition/): Configure graceful leader transitions in Kubernetes 1.36 control plane components. Eliminate brief outages during leader election failovers.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 L3 Cache Topology in CPU Manager](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-1-36-l3-cache-topology-cpu-manager/): Configure L3 cache topology awareness in Kubernetes 1.36 CPU Manager. Allocate CPUs sharing L3 cache for better performance in latency-sensitive workloads.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 Memory QoS with cgroups v2](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-1-36-memory-qos-cgroups-v2/): Configure memory quality of service with cgroups v2 in Kubernetes 1.36. Set memory.min and memory.high for guaranteed memory and throttling before OOM kills.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 Mixed Version Proxy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-1-36-mixed-version-proxy/): Use the Mixed Version Proxy in Kubernetes 1.36 to handle API version skew during rolling upgrades. Ensures API availability across mixed control plane versions.
- [Kubernetes 1.36 Pod-Level Resource Limits](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-1-36-pod-level-resources/): Set resource requests and limits at the Pod level in Kubernetes 1.36 instead of per-container. Simplifies multi-container Pod resource management.

- [Set Kernel Parameters via MachineConfig](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/machineconfig-kernel-parameters/): Tune kernel sysctl parameters on OpenShift nodes using MachineConfig. Set networking, memory, and performance sysctls on RHCOS.
- [Configure Container Registries via MachineC...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/machineconfig-registries-conf/): Set up mirror registries and blocked registries on OpenShift nodes using MachineConfig to control CRI-O image pull on RHCOS.
- [Configure MCP maxUnavailable for Rollouts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-maxunavailable-configuration/): Control how many nodes the MachineConfig Operator updates simultaneously. Set maxUnavailable for faster rollouts or safer one-at-a-time updates in production.
- [Pause and Unpause MCP Rollouts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-pause-unpause-rollout/): Temporarily pause MachineConfigPool rollouts to batch multiple MachineConfig changes or coordinate with maintenance windows. Unpause to resume node updates.
- [Automate MCP Updates with Drain Script](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-update-automation-script/): Bash script to automate OpenShift MachineConfigPool updates when drains are blocked by PDB violations. Auto-detects blockers, scales down, drains, and restores.
- [Separate Worker and Infra MachineConfigPools](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-worker-infra-separation/): Create dedicated MachineConfigPools for infrastructure and GPU nodes. Isolate MCP rollout blast radius and control update order for different node types.
- [Mirror OpenShift Releases to Disconnected Registry](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mirror-openshift-releases-disconnected-registry/): Mirror OCP release images to an air-gapped Quay registry using oc adm release mirror. Auth setup, proxy config, ImageDigestMirrorSet, and disconnected updates.
- [MOFED and DOCA Driver Building for OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mofed-doca-driver-building-openshift/): Build NVIDIA MOFED and DOCA drivers for OpenShift using DriverToolKit, Buildah, and MachineConfig for RDMA and GPU networking.
- [How to Manage K8s Namespaces Effectively](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/namespace-management/): Master Kubernetes namespace organization for multi-team environments. Learn resource quotas, network policies, and RBAC per namespace.
- [NicClusterPolicy MOFED Affinity & Node Sele...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/nicclusterpolicy-node-selection/): Configure NicClusterPolicy node selectors and affinity rules to deploy MOFED drivers only on RDMA-capable nodes in Kubernetes clusters.
- [How to Implement K8s Taints and Tolerations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/node-taints-tolerations/): Control pod scheduling with taints and tolerations. Dedicate nodes for specific workloads, handle node conditions, and implement scheduling constraints.
- [Use oc adm drain Dry-Run for Diagnostics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/oc-adm-drain-dry-run-diagnostics/): Preview node drain impact without evicting pods. Identify PDB violations, unmanaged pods, and local storage blockers before maintenance.
- [OCI Container Image Internals on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/oci-container-image-internals-kubernetes/): Understand OCI container image internals: layers as tar archive diffs, image configuration JSON, content-addressable storage with SHA-256, multi-platform image
- [Open Kernel Modules and DMA-BUF for GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/open-kernel-modules-dma-buf/): Migrate from proprietary NVIDIA kernel modules and nvidia-peermem to open kernel modules with DMA-BUF for safer GPU upgrades.
- [OpenClaw Cron Jobs and Heartbeats](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-cron-heartbeat-kubernetes/): Configure OpenClaw's built-in cron scheduling and heartbeat system on Kubernetes for proactive notifications, periodic checks, and automated background.
- [OpenClaw Multi-Model Provider Setup](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-multi-model-providers-kubernetes/): Configure OpenClaw with multiple AI providers on Kubernetes. Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, OpenRouter with fallback chains and cost control.
- [OpenClaw Node Pairing for IoT and Edge Devices](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-node-pairing-kubernetes/): Pair phones, Raspberry Pi, and edge devices with OpenClaw on Kubernetes. Camera, location, screen control, and remote command execution.
- [Manage OpenClaw Skills on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-skills-kubernetes/): Deploy and manage OpenClaw agent skills (tools, automations, integrations) on Kubernetes using ConfigMaps, PVCs, and git-sync for dynamic capability.
- [GitOps for OpenClaw Workspaces on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openclaw-workspace-gitops/): Manage OpenClaw agent workspaces (SOUL.md, skills, memory) with GitOps using Flux or ArgoCD, enabling version-controlled AI persona management on.
- [Create Custom CatalogSources for OLM Operators](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-catalogsource-custom-operator/): Configure CatalogSource in OpenShift to serve custom operator catalogs from private registries or air-gapped environments.
- [OpenShift Cluster Update Process Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-cluster-update-process-explained/): Complete guide to OpenShift Container Platform cluster updates. CVO workflow, Runlevels, Machine Config Operator node updates, update channels
- [OpenShift IDMS ITMS Mirror Rules Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-idms-itms-mirror-rules/): Configure IDMS and ITMS mirror rules in OpenShift for disconnected registries. NeverContactSource vs AllowContactingSource and ICSP migration.
- [OpenShift Lifecycle and Version Support](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-lifecycle-versions/): OpenShift support lifecycle guide covering version support phases, EUS releases, end-of-life dates, and upgrade planning for production clusters.
- [OpenShift Lifecycle Versions Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-lifecycle-versions-guide/): OpenShift Container Platform lifecycle, version support, and upgrade planning. EUS versions, support timelines, K8s version mapping, and EOL dates.
- [OpenShift MachineConfig and MCP Deep Dive](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-machineconfig-mcp-guide/): Master MachineConfig and MachineConfigPool on OpenShift. Configure kernel args, files, systemd units, and manage rolling node updates with MCP strategies.
- [Cordon, Drain, and Uncordon Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-node-cordon-uncordon/): Safely remove workloads from OpenShift and Kubernetes nodes for maintenance. Cordon to prevent scheduling, drain to evict pods, uncordon to restore.
- [Disable OperatorHub Default Sources](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-operatorhub-disable-sources/): Disable default OperatorHub catalog sources in OpenShift for air-gapped clusters. Use OperatorHub CR to disable individual or all sources with Ansible auto.
- [OpenShift Project Request Template Pull Sec...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-project-request-template/): Configure an OpenShift Project Request Template so every new namespace automatically gets a ServiceAccount with imagePullSecrets for your private Quay registry.
- [Configure PDBs for OpenShift Routers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-router-pdb-configuration/): Set PodDisruptionBudgets for OpenShift IngressController routers. Balance availability during maintenance with node drain ability.
- [OpenShift Support Lifecycle and Version Matrix](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-support-lifecycle-version-matrix/): OpenShift Container Platform support lifecycle, version EOL dates, Kubernetes version mapping, upgrade paths, and Extended Update Support (EUS). Plan upgrades
- [OpenShift Upgrade Planning for 2026](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/openshift-upgrade-planning-2026/): Plan OpenShift upgrades for 2026. EUS-to-EUS paths, operator compatibility, pre-upgrade checks, canary node pools, and rollback strategy for OCP 4.14 to 4.18.
- [OSUS Direct vs Replicated OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/osus-direct-vs-replicated-openshift/): Choose between direct and replicated OSUS graph data modes in OpenShift. Configure UpdateService for connected and disconnected environments.
- [How to Use Pod Presets and Mutations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/pod-mutation-injection/): Automatically inject configurations into pods using admission controllers. Configure environment variables, volumes, and annotations at deployment time.
- [How to Configure Pod Resource Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/pod-resource-management/): Set CPU and memory requests and limits effectively. Understand QoS classes, resource quotas, and optimize container resource allocation.
- [PriorityClasses for GPU Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/priorityclasses-gpu-workloads/): Configure Kubernetes PriorityClasses for GPU workloads with training, serving, batch, and interactive tiers and preemption policies.
- [Record kubectl Sessions for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/record-kubectl-sessions-kubernetes/): Record and replay kubectl sessions for auditing, documentation, and training. Terminal recording with asciinema, script, and kubectl plugins for OpenShift.
- [K8s Resource Limits: CPU 500m Memory 256Mi](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-limits-requests/): Configure Kubernetes container resource limits and requests. CPU 200m/500m, memory 256Mi syntax and format explained with QoS classes and right-sizing.
- [How to Configure Resource Quotas](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-quotas/): Limit resource consumption per namespace with ResourceQuotas. Control CPU, memory, storage, and object counts to ensure fair cluster sharing.
- [How to Configure Resource Quotas per Namespace](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-quotas-namespace/): Implement resource quotas to limit CPU, memory, and object counts per namespace. Ensure fair resource allocation across teams and environments.
- [Set Resource Requests and Limits Properly](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-requests-limits/): Master Kubernetes resource management with proper CPU and memory requests and limits. Avoid OOMKills, throttling, and resource contention.
- [ResourceQuota and LimitRange for GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resourcequota-limitrange-gpu/): Configure ResourceQuota and LimitRange for GPU workloads with per-tenant caps on GPU, CPU, memory, and object counts in Kubernetes.
- [Restore Scaled Deployments After Node Drain](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/restore-scaled-deployments-after-drain/): Restore deployments scaled down for maintenance. Verify node health, check pod scheduling, and confirm service availability.
- [RHCOS Node Management for OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/rhcos-openshift-node-management/): Understand and manage Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS (RHCOS) for OpenShift nodes including MachineConfig, ignition, OS updates, and node customization.
- [Scale Deployments to Unblock Node Drains](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/scale-deploy-unblock-drain/): Safely scale down deployments that block node drains due to PDB violations. Record original replicas, scale to zero, drain, then restore after the node returns.
- [Kubernetes Scheduler Configuration and Tuning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/scheduler-configuration-tuning/): Customize the Kubernetes scheduler with scheduling profiles, plugins, and advanced placement strategies for optimal pod placement and resource utilization
- [Switch to Open NVIDIA Kernel Modules on Ope...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/switch-proprietary-to-open-kernel-modules/): Step-by-step guide to migrate the NVIDIA GPU Operator from proprietary to open kernel modules on OpenShift, enabling DMA-BUF and GPUDirect Storage support.
- [Tune NCCL Env Variables for RDMA & Ethernet](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/tune-nccl-env-rdma-ethernet/): Apply safe NCCL environment variable profiles for RDMA-capable and Ethernet-only GPU clusters to maximize collective communication throughput.

## Deployments (249 recipes)

- [How to Implement A/B Testing with Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/ab-testing-kubernetes/): Route traffic between application versions for A/B testing. Use service mesh, ingress, and custom routing rules to validate features with real users.
- [Air-Gapped Image Import for OpenShift Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/air-gapped-image-import-openshift/): Import container images into disconnected OpenShift clusters. Use podman save/load and internal registries when DNS and TLS block external pulls.

- [Argo Workflows on Kubernetes: CI/CD Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argo-workflows-kubernetes/): Run CI/CD pipelines and data workflows with Argo Workflows on Kubernetes. Create DAG-based workflows, parallel steps, artifact passing, and cron workflows.
- [ArgoCD App of Apps Pattern Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-pattern/): Implement the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern to manage multiple applications from a parent Application for cluster bootstrapping.

- [ArgoCD ApplicationSets for Multi-Tenant GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-applicationsets-multi-tenant/): Use ArgoCD ApplicationSets to auto-discover and provision GPU tenant overlays from Git directories with per-tenant sync policies.

- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for Canary Deployments](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-canary/): Use ArgoCD sync waves for canary deployments with Istio traffic splitting, automated validation, and progressive rollout strategies.
- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for CRD & Operator Ordering](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-crd-operators/): Use ArgoCD sync waves to deploy Custom Resource Definitions before operators and custom resources, preventing CRD race conditions in GitOps pipelines.
- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for Database Migrations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-database-migration/): Use ArgoCD sync waves and PreSync hooks to run database migrations before deploying application code, with rollback strategies.
- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for Ordered Deployments](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-ordering/): Use ArgoCD sync waves to control the order of Kubernetes resource deployment, ensuring dependencies like namespaces and CRDs are created before workloads.
- [Automate NCCL Preflight Checks in CI/CD Pipelines](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/automate-nccl-preflight-ci/): Run NCCL smoke benchmarks automatically in CI/CD pipelines before promoting GPU cluster changes to production, catching regressions early.
- [Backstage Developer Portal on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/backstage-kubernetes-developer-portal/): Deploy Spotify Backstage on Kubernetes as an internal developer portal. Covers Helm install, PostgreSQL backend, catalog entities, and TechDocs integration.
- [How to Implement Blue-Green Deployments](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/blue-green-deployment/): Learn how to implement blue-green deployments in Kubernetes for instant rollbacks and zero-downtime releases. Complete guide with Service switching.

- [Install Kubernetes on RHEL: Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/install-kubernetes-rhel/): Step-by-step guide to install Kubernetes on RHEL with kubeadm. Covers containerd, kubeadm init, CNI setup, and worker node joining for RHEL 8/9.
- [Install Kubernetes on Rocky Linux](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/install-kubernetes-rocky-linux/): Step-by-step guide to install Kubernetes on Rocky Linux with kubeadm. Covers containerd, kubeadm init, CNI setup, and worker node joining for Rocky Linux 8/9.

- [Install Kubernetes on Ubuntu: Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/install-kubernetes-ubuntu/): Step-by-step guide to install Kubernetes on Ubuntu with kubeadm. Covers containerd, kubeadm init, CNI setup, and worker node joining for Ubuntu 22.04/24.04.

- [kubectl get pods: Output Formats Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubectl-get-pods-examples/): Master kubectl get pods with output formats, label selectors, field selectors, and custom columns. Wide output, JSON, YAML, and jsonpath examples.

- [How to Use Multi-Container Pod Patterns](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/multi-container-pod-patterns/): Master Kubernetes multi-container pod patterns including sidecar, ambassador, and adapter. Learn when and how to use each pattern for microservices.
- [Mutagen Kubernetes File Sync Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/mutagen-kubernetes-file-sync/): Sync files between local machine and Kubernetes pods with Mutagen. Real-time bidirectional sync for development, hot-reload workflows.
- [oc-mirror Plugin Disconnected OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/oc-mirror-disconnected-openshift/): Use oc-mirror to mirror OpenShift content for disconnected installations. ImageSetConfiguration, incremental mirrors, and operator catalog mirroring.
- [Octopus Deploy 2025.4 on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/octopus-deploy-kubernetes-agent/): Deploy Octopus Deploy 2025.4 with MSSQL and Kubernetes agent on k3s. Release orchestration with ephemeral preview environments, approval gates.
- [OLM Disconnected OpenShift Operators](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/olm-disconnected-openshift/): Use Operator Lifecycle Manager in disconnected OpenShift clusters. Mirror catalogs, create CatalogSources, and manage Operators without internet access.
- [OpenClaw GitOps Deployment with ArgoCD](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/openclaw-argocd-gitops/): Deploy OpenClaw on Kubernetes using ArgoCD for GitOps automation. Application definition, sync policies, drift detection, and secrets.

- [How to Create Helm Charts from Scratch](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-chart-creation/): Build custom Helm charts for your applications. Learn chart structure, templates, values, dependencies, and best practices for packaging Kubernetes.

- [Helm Hooks and Lifecycle Management Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-hooks-lifecycle-guide/): Master Helm hooks for Kubernetes deployments. Pre-install, post-install, pre-upgrade, hook weights, deletion policies, and database migration patterns.
- [Helm Install: Deploy Charts Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-install-chart-guide/): Install Helm charts on Kubernetes with helm install, upgrade, rollback, and values customization. Repository management, OCI registries, and release lifecycle.
- [Helm Library Charts for Reusable Templates](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-library-charts/): Create Helm library charts to share common templates across multiple charts. DRY up deployments, services, and config patterns with reusable library functions.
- [Helm OCI Registry for Charts Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-oci-registry-charts/): Store and manage Helm charts in OCI-compliant registries like GHCR, ECR, ACR, and Quay. Push, pull, and version charts using standard container registries.
- [Helm OCI Registry for Chart Distribution](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-oci-registry-management/): Store and distribute Helm charts using OCI registries like GHCR, ECR, ACR, and Harbor. Migrate from ChartMuseum to OCI-native chart management.
- [Helm Rollback and History Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-rollback-history-guide/): Roll back Helm releases and manage revision history. Diagnose failed upgrades, compare revisions, and automate rollback.
- [Helm Secrets Mgmt with SOPS & Age Encryption](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-secrets-sops-management/): Encrypt Helm values files using SOPS with Age or GPG keys. Manage secrets in Git safely with helm-secrets plugin for transparent encrypt/decrypt workflows.
- [Helm Sprig add1 trim merge Functions](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-sprig-add1-trim-merge-functions/): Helm Sprig add1 function increments integers in templates. Plus trim for whitespace removal and merge for combining dictionaries in Helm charts.
- [Helm Sprig cat Function: Concatenate Strings](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-sprig-cat-function/): Helm Sprig cat function concatenates strings with spaces between arguments. Syntax, why cat inserts spaces, conditionals, and template examples.
- [Helm Sprig Functions Complete Reference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-sprig-functions-complete-reference/): Complete reference for Helm Sprig template functions including cat, print, join, tostring, add1, trim, quote, default, and more. Examples and common patterns
- [Helm Sprig join Function: List to String](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-sprig-join-function/): Helm Sprig join function converts lists to delimited strings. Join list example with CSV output, label values, and multi-value template patterns.
- [Helm Sprig print quote default Functions](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-sprig-print-quote-default-functions/): Helm Sprig print function concatenates without spaces, quote wraps in double quotes, default provides fallback values. Template examples and patterns.
- [Helm Sprig toString Function Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-sprig-tostring-function/): Helm Sprig toString function converts values to strings in templates. Handle integers, booleans, lists, and nil values safely in Helm charts.
- [Helm Sprig Functions: cat, print, toString](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-templating-sprig/): Master Helm Sprig functions: cat, print, toString, add1, join, and quote. String manipulation, conditionals, and advanced templating patterns.
- [Fix Helm Upgrade Failed and Rollback](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-upgrade-failed-troubleshooting/): Debug failed Helm releases stuck in pending-upgrade or failed state. Covers atomic upgrades, manual rollback, secret storage cleanup, and history limits.
- [Helm Values Override Patterns Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/helm/helm-values-override-patterns/): Master Helm values override patterns. CLI flags, multiple files, JSON values, and precedence rules for complex deployments.

- [How to Customize DNS Configuration in K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/custom-dns-configuration/): Configure custom DNS settings in Kubernetes. Learn CoreDNS customization, stub domains, upstream servers, and pod DNS policies.
- [DCB on Mellanox ConnectX: Lossless Ethernet...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/dcb-mellanox-connectx-lossless-ethernet/): Configure Data Center Bridging (DCB) on Mellanox ConnectX NICs. DCBX negotiation, PFC, ETS, and CN for lossless RoCE Ethernet in Kubernetes AI clusters.
- [Dell Switch RoCEv2 PFC ECN DSCP](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/dell-switch-rocev2-pfc-ecn-dscp/): Configure Dell OS10 switches for lossless RoCEv2 with PFC, ECN, WRED, and DSCP-to-traffic-class mapping. Priority 3 for RDMA traffic classes 24 and 26.
- [How to Configure Kubernetes DNS Policies](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/dns-policies-configuration/): Control pod DNS resolution with DNS policies and configs. Configure custom nameservers, search domains, and optimize DNS for your workloads.
- [Dual-Fabric Mellanox: GPU InfiniBand + Storage Ethernet](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/dual-fabric-mellanox-gpu-storage-ethernet-infiniband/): Design and configure dual-fabric network architecture with separate Mellanox NICs for GPU communication (InfiniBand) and storage traffic (Ethernet). Covers
- [ECN MachineConfig OpenShift Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/ecn-machineconfig-openshift-nodes/): Enable ECN (Explicit Congestion Notification) on OpenShift nodes via MachineConfig for lossless RoCEv2 RDMA networking. Sysctl and Mellanox NIC configuration.
- [Enable NIC Feature Discovery in NVIDIA Netw...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/enable-nic-feature-discovery/): Enable NIC Feature Discovery through NicClusterPolicy and verify the node labels required by SR-IOV and RDMA GPU networking workflows on Kubernetes.
- [ETS Queue, PFC, DSCP Trust on Mellanox Quic...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/ets-pfc-dscp-trust-mellanox-quickstart/): Quick reference for enabling ETS queues, PFC, DSCP trust, and DSCP-to-priority mapping on Mellanox ConnectX NICs. Three commands for lossless RoCE Ethernet.
- [External DNS for Kubernetes: Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/external-dns-kubernetes/): Automate DNS record management with ExternalDNS for Kubernetes. Sync Service and Ingress hostnames to Route53, CloudFlare, Google Cloud DNS, and 30+ providers.
- [Gateway API HTTPRoutes TLS on k3s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/gateway-api-httproutes-tls-k3s/): Configure Gateway API HTTPRoutes with TLS termination on k3s using Cilium. Route traffic to multiple services with wildcard certificates and HTTP-to-HTTPS .
- [GPUDirect RDMA via DMA-BUF on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/gpudirect-rdma-dma-buf/): Configure GPUDirect RDMA using DMA-BUF kernel subsystem for zero-copy GPU-to-GPU transfers over InfiniBand and RoCE networks.
- [HAProxy Keepalived Multi-Tenant GPU Ingress](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/haproxy-keepalived-multi-tenant-gpu/): Configure HAProxy with Keepalived VIPs for per-tenant GPU cluster ingress with Jinja2 templates and per-tenant access logging.
- [ib_write_bw RDMA Bandwidth Testing](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/ib-write-bw-rdma-benchmark-kubernetes/): Run ib_write_bw from perftest on Kubernetes to measure RDMA write bandwidth between GPU nodes. Full CLI reference, bidirectional tests, HugePages.
- [InfiniBand vs Ethernet for AI on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/infiniband-ethernet-ai-kubernetes/): Compare InfiniBand and Ethernet networking for GPU AI workloads on Kubernetes, including RDMA, RoCE, latency, and throughput considerations.

- [NFSoRDMA Bond with Access Mode Switch](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nfsordma-bond-access-mode/): Configure bonded NICs for NFS over RDMA using switch access mode for VLAN assignment. Aggregation on untagged interfaces for RDMA redundancy.
- [NFSoRDMA Dedicated NIC Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nfsordma-dedicated-nic/): Configure dedicated NICs for NFS over RDMA on Kubernetes worker nodes. NFSoRDMA requires untagged interfaces — no VLAN tagging supported.
- [NFSoRDMA Jumbo Frames MTU Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nfsordma-jumbo-frames-mtu/): Configure 9000 MTU jumbo frames for NFSoRDMA interfaces using NNCP to maximize RDMA throughput on Kubernetes worker nodes.

- [NMState Network Config for GPU Worker Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nmstate-ethernet-bond-vlan-gpu-nodes/): Declaratively configure Ethernet bonding, VLANs, MTU, and static routes on GPU worker nodes using NMState on OpenShift. Covers bonding modes, LACP
- [NMState & nmstatectl: Node Network Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nmstate-nmstatectl-node-network-guide/): Manage node networking with NMState declarative API and nmstatectl CLI. Create NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy manifests, verify with nmstatectl.
- [NMState Operator Install OpenShift K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nmstate-operator-install-openshift/): Install and configure the NMState operator on OpenShift and Kubernetes. Enable declarative node networking with NNCP, NodeNetworkState, and enactments.
- [NNCP Bond Interfaces on Worker Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-bond-interfaces-workers/): Create bonded network interfaces on Kubernetes worker nodes using NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy for NIC redundancy and link aggregation.
- [NNCP DNS and Static Routes on Workers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-dns-routes-workers/): Configure static routes, DNS servers, and policy-based routing on worker nodes using NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy for multi-network setups.
- [NNCP Linux Bridge on Worker Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-linux-bridge-workers/): Create Linux bridges on Kubernetes worker nodes using NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy for KubeVirt VM networking and pod bridging.
- [NNCP MTU and Jumbo Frames on Workers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-mtu-jumbo-frames-workers/): Set MTU and enable jumbo frames on worker node interfaces using NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy for high-throughput storage and AI networking.
- [NNCP Multi-NIC Architecture for Workers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-multi-nic-workers/): Design a complete multi-NIC worker node architecture with NNCP for separated management, storage, tenant, and GPU traffic using bonds, VLANs, and bridges.
- [NNCP NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-nodenetworkconfigurationpolicy-guide/): Master NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy (NNCP) on OpenShift and Kubernetes. Configure VLANs, bonds, bridges, SR-IOV, MTU, static IPs, and DNS with NMState.
- [NNCP OVS Bridge on Worker Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-ovs-bridge-workers/): Configure Open vSwitch bridges on Kubernetes worker nodes using NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy for advanced SDN and DPDK networking.
- [NNCP Rollback and Troubleshooting](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-rollback-troubleshooting/): Troubleshoot NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy failures, monitor enactments, configure rollback timeouts, and recover from bad network configurations.
- [NNCP SR-IOV and Macvlan on Workers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-sriov-macvlan-workers/): Configure SR-IOV virtual functions and macvlan interfaces on worker nodes using NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy for high-performance networking.
- [NNCP Static IP Assignment on Worker Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-static-ip-workers/): Use NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy to assign static IPv4 and IPv6 addresses to worker node interfaces with nodeSelector targeting.
- [NNCP VLAN Tagging on Worker Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nncp-vlan-tagging-workers/): Configure VLAN interfaces on Kubernetes worker nodes using NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy for network segmentation and traffic isolation.
- [NodePort Raw Traffic vs HTTPS Ingress](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nodeport-raw-vs-https-ingress/): Route raw GPU inference traffic via NodePort for low-latency gRPC and HTTPS model serving via OpenShift ingress controller.
- [NVIDIA IPAM for GPU Fabric IP Address Allocation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nv-ipam-gpu-fabric-ip-allocation/): Configure nv-ipam (NVIDIA IPAM) to assign IP addresses on GPU fabric SR-IOV networks in Kubernetes. Covers IPPool CRDs, per-node allocation, InfiniBand IPoIB
- [NVIDIA Network Operator NicClusterPolicy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nvidia-network-operator-nicclusterpolicy/): Deploy NVIDIA Network Operator on OpenShift with NicClusterPolicy for DOCA telemetry, NIC feature discovery, RDMA IPAM, and OFED drivers. GitOps-managed
- [RDMA Configuration with NVIDIA Network Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nvidia-network-operator-rdma-kubernetes/): Deploy and configure RDMA for GPU clusters using the NVIDIA Network Operator. NicClusterPolicy setup, MLNX_OFED driver container, shared and SR-IOV RDMA device
- [NVIDIA NIC Driver Container Entrypoint](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/nvidia-nic-driver-container-entrypoint/): Understand and customize the NVIDIA NIC driver container entrypoint for MOFED and DOCA driver lifecycle on Kubernetes and OpenShift.
- [Expose OpenClaw via K8s Ingress with TLS](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/openclaw-ingress-tls-kubernetes/): Configure Kubernetes Ingress with TLS to expose OpenClaw gateway securely. Covers cert-manager, NGINX Ingress, and allowed origins.
- [OpenShift Multus CNI Multiple Network Interfaces](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/openshift-multus-cni-multiple-networks/): Attach multiple network interfaces to Pods using Multus CNI on OpenShift. Covers NetworkAttachmentDefinitions, SR-IOV, macvlan, IPVLAN, traffic separation
- [OpenShift Routes vs Ingress Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/openshift-routes-vs-ingress/): Compare OpenShift Routes and Kubernetes Ingress. Covers edge, passthrough, and re-encrypt TLS termination, and when to use each option.
- [Fix SR-IOV 'Not Enough MMIO Resources' Error](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/openshift-sriov-mmio-resources-fix/): Resolve the mlx5_core 'not enough MMIO resources for SR-IOV' error on OpenShift nodes with Mellanox ConnectX NICs. Covers BIOS settings, PCIe BAR
- [OpenShift SR-IOV Network with NVIDIA IPAM for GPU Fabric](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/openshift-sriov-network-nv-ipam-gpu-fabric/): Configure SriovNetwork resources on OpenShift with nv-ipam for GPU fabric IP allocation. SR-IOV Network Operator setup, Mellanox NIC resource targeting, IPAM
- [OpenShift SR-IOV RDMA InfiniBand Device Plugin](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/openshift-sriov-rdma-infiniband-device-plugin/): Configure and troubleshoot SR-IOV Network Operator with Mellanox ConnectX RDMA InfiniBand devices on OpenShift. Covers SriovNetworkNodePolicy, device
- [Create SR-IOV VFs on OpenShift SriovNetwork...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/openshift-sriov-vf-creation/): Use the OpenShift SR-IOV Network Operator to create and manage Virtual Functions from selected Physical Functions on GPU worker nodes.
- [PFC Configuration on Mellanox ConnectX NICs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/pfc-mellanox-connectx-configuration/): Enable Priority Flow Control on Mellanox ConnectX-6/7 NICs for lossless RoCE. mlnx_qos, cma_roce_mode, DSCP trust, ECN, and firmware-level PFC verification.
- [Configure PFC with NMState on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/pfc-nmstate-roce-lossless-kubernetes/): Enable Priority Flow Control (PFC) for lossless RDMA using NMState and NodeNetworkConfigurationPolicy. Configure DSCP-to-priority mapping, ECN, and RoCEv2 QoS.
- [Manage hostNetwork Pod Port Allocation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/pod-hostnetwork-port-mapping/): Plan and manage host port usage for hostNetwork pods. Prevent port conflicts, track allocations, and handle port exhaustion.
- [Kubernetes Rate Limiting with NGINX and Istio](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/rate-limiting-kubernetes/): Implement Kubernetes rate limiting for API protection. Ingress NGINX annotations, Istio rate limits, Kong plugins, and per-service rate limiting patterns.
- [RDMA Network QoS Traffic Classes DCQCN](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/rdma-network-qos-traffic-classes-dcqcn/): Complete RDMA network QoS architecture with traffic classes TC0-TC6, DSCP and dot1p mappings, PFC, ECN, WRED, and DCQCN congestion control for lossless RoC.
- [RoCE PFC and ECN Lossless Ethernet for GPU Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/roce-pfc-ecn-lossless-ethernet-gpu/): Configure RoCE v2 with Priority Flow Control (PFC) and ECN for lossless Ethernet RDMA on GPU clusters. Covers DSCP mapping, switch configuration, NIC
- [RoCEv2 End-to-End Lossless Stack](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/rocev2-end-to-end-lossless-stack/): Complete RoCEv2 lossless fabric configuration from GPU node to switch and back. Dell OS10 switches, Mellanox NICs, OpenShift MachineConfig, PFC, ECN.
- [Expose Services with LoadBalancer and NodePort](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/service-loadbalancer-nodeport/): Learn different ways to expose Kubernetes services externally using LoadBalancer, NodePort, and ExternalIPs. Compare options for various environments.
- [Shared RDMA Device Plugin for Kubernetes GPU Pods](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/shared-rdma-device-plugin-kubernetes/): Configure the RDMA shared device plugin to allow multiple pods to share RDMA-capable NICs on Kubernetes. K8s-rdma-shared-dev-plugin setup, resource
- [SR-IOV Device Plugin PF Flag on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/sriov-device-plugin-pf-flag-kubernetes/): Configure SR-IOV device plugin PF flag in Kubernetes. Expose physical functions as allocatable resources for exclusive RDMA access.
- [SR-IOV Mixed NICs for GPU Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/sriov-mixed-nic-gpu-nodes/): Configure SR-IOV with mixed ConnectX-7 and ConnectX-6 NICs for RDMA data plane and management traffic on GPU worker nodes.

- [SR-IOV NetworkNodePolicy for RDMA](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/sriov-network-node-policy-rdma-openshift/): Configure SriovNetworkNodePolicy on OpenShift to create RDMA-capable VFs on Mellanox ConnectX NICs for GPUDirect RDMA and high-performance AI networking.
- [SR-IOV NicClusterPolicy for VF Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/sriov-nicclusterpolicy-vfs/): Configure SR-IOV Virtual Functions on Mellanox ConnectX NICs using the NVIDIA Network Operator NicClusterPolicy for high-performance Kubernetes networking.

- [SR-IOV VF to Container Mapping and Lifecycle](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/sriov-vf-container-mapping-lifecycle/): How SR-IOV Virtual Functions are mapped to containers in Kubernetes. Covers VF allocation flow, link state management (VFs are down when unassigned), device
- [Switch GPUDirect RDMA from nvidia-peermem t...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/switch-gpudirect-rdma-dma-buf/): Migrate from the legacy nvidia-peermem kernel module to the recommended DMA-BUF GPUDirect RDMA path using the NVIDIA GPU Operator.
- [Validate GPUDirect RDMA Performance DMA-BUF](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/validate-gpudirect-rdma-performance/): Run ib_write_bw with CUDA DMA-BUF to verify GPUDirect RDMA data transfer rates between GPU pods and validate network operator configuration.
- [Verify Which Interface Carries OVN Underlay...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/verify-ovn-underlay-interface/): Confirm the actual OVN underlay network path by checking ovn-encap-ip, bridge port ownership, and physical route associations on Kubernetes nodes.
- [VT-x vs VT-d vs SR-IOV Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/networking/virtualization-vt-x-vs-vt-d-vs-sriov/): Understand the difference between CPU virtualization (VT-x/SVM), I/O virtualization (VT-d/AMD-Vi/IOMMU), and SR-IOV. Which to enable or disable for GPU
